im kinda good at crochetting. i made a blanket for my cat

It would be worse. It hurts a fuck ton to be shattered into a million pieces. But if you don’t learn to accept and understand the situation that occured in the past. Then it will forever stagnate in your mind, rendering you unable to from any deeper bonds with people. You dont need to forgive, but you need to accept it occured, to grow and be better. It’s not easy, I never gets easier with each betrayal. However, if you choose to close your true self from others, you will always feel alone, mentally. Unable to share who you are and what you think. Unable to find people who care for who you truly are, no matter what, your true self, even when you are at your lowest.

I didn’t say you couldn’t wear it, I said you have to take it off.

There was a time where I wore so many different masks, all thinking they were different people and not a part of who I am. I’ve come to accept these masks aren’t fake, but rather different parts of my personality. I can be a silly joker sometimes, but a depressed fuck, and other times a relaxed chill vibe to be around.
Still me at the end of the day~

“Do you know the story of the monkeys of the shitty island?" I asked my friend. 
He shook his head, with no sign of interest. "Never heard of it".
"Somewhere, far, far away, there's a shitty island. An island without a name. An island not worth giving a name. A shitty island with shitty shape. On this shitty island grow palm trees that also have shitty shapes. And the palm trees produce coconuts that give off a shitty smell. Shitty monkeys live in the trees, and they love to eat these shitty-smelling coconuts, after which they shit the world's foulest shit. The shit falls on the ground and builds up shitty mounds, making the shitty palm trees that grow on them even shittier. It's an endless cycle."
I drank the rest of 40loko
"As I sat here looking at you," I continued, " I suddenly remembered the story of this shitty island. What I'm trying to say is this. A certain kind of shittiness, a certain kind of stagnation, a certain kind of darkness, goes on propagating itself by its own power in its own self-contained cycle. And once it passed a certain point, no one can stop it -even if the person himself want to stop it.”
― Haruki Murakami, The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
